title: Executive communication
subtitle: Коммуникация с руководством: board, C-level emails
content:
Общение на уровне руководства требует точного и лаконичного языка.

Key phrases:
- I'd like to bring to your attention... — Хочу обратить ваше внимание...
- The bottom line is... — Суть в том, что...
- Going forward, we plan to... — В дальнейшем мы планируем...
- I'd recommend we prioritize... — Рекомендую приоритизировать...
- To summarize the key takeaways... — Подводя итоги ключевых выводов...

Board presentation: executive summary, action items, key metrics, risk assessment.

keyVocabulary.example: The key takeaway from this meeting is...
The bottom line is we need more resources.
I need to escalate this issue.
I don't have bandwidth for this.

defaultCards.front: I'd like to bring to your attention...
The bottom line is we need more budget.
defaultCards.back: Суть в том, что нам нужно больше бюджета.

defaultCards.front: I don't have ___ for this task.

title: Technical architecture
subtitle: Microservices, monolith, event-driven architecture
content:
Урок C1: Technical architecture. Microservices, monolith, event-driven architecture.

• microservices — микросервисы
• monolith — монолит
• event-driven — событийно-ориентированный
• scalability — масштабируемость
• load balancer — балансировщик нагрузки
• fault tolerance — отказоустойчивость
• distributed system — распределённая система
• decoupling — разделение зависимостей
• API gateway — API шлюз
• service mesh — сервисная сетка

keyVocabulary.example: The legacy system is a monolith.
The system has built-in fault tolerance.
All requests go through the API gateway.

defaultCards.context: The legacy system is a monolith.
The system has built-in fault tolerance.
All requests go through the API gateway.
The legacy system is a monolith.

title: System design interviews
subtitle: Scalability, load balancing, caching strategies
content:
Урок C1: System design interviews. Scalability, load balancing, caching strategies.

• throughput — пропускная способность
• latency — задержка
• caching — кэширование
• sharding — шардирование
• replication — репликация
• rate limiting — ограничение частоты запросов
• horizontal scaling — горизонтальное масштабирование
• CAP theorem — теорема CAP
• eventual consistency — согласованность в конечном счёте
• bottleneck — узкое место

keyVocabulary.example: We need to reduce latency to under 100ms.
We shard the database by user ID.
We scale horizontally by adding more nodes.
The CAP theorem defines distributed trade-offs.
We use eventual consistency for this service.
The database is the main bottleneck.

grammarPoints: Контекст: Scalability, load balancing, caching strategies

defaultCards.context: We need to reduce latency to under 100ms.
We shard the database by user ID.
We scale horizontally by adding more nodes.
The CAP theorem defines distributed trade-offs.
We use eventual consistency for this service.
The database is the main bottleneck.
We need to reduce latency to under 100ms.
We shard the database by user ID.

title: Code review best practices
subtitle: Constructive feedback, PR comments
content:
Урок C1: Code review best practices. Constructive feedback, PR comments.

• pull request — запрос на слияние
• code review — ревью кода
• nit — мелкое замечание
• LGTM — выглядит хорошо (одобряю)
• refactor — рефакторинг
• technical debt — технический долг
• edge case — граничный случай
• regression — регрессия
• merge conflict — конфликт слияния
• CI pipeline — конвейер CI

keyVocabulary.example: Code review is mandatory before merging.
We need to address technical debt.
Did you handle this edge case?
This change might cause a regression.
I need to resolve a merge conflict.
The CI pipeline failed on this PR.

cardGenerationHints.prompt: Cards about Code review best practices

defaultCards.context: Code review is mandatory before merging.
We need to address technical debt.
Did you handle this edge case?
This change might cause a regression.
I need to resolve a merge conflict.
The CI pipeline failed on this PR.
Code review is mandatory before merging.

title: Incident management
subtitle: Postmortem, root cause analysis, SLA breach
content:
Урок C1: Incident management. Postmortem, root cause analysis, SLA breach.

• incident — инцидент
• postmortem — разбор инцидента
• root cause — первопричина
• SLA breach — нарушение SLA
• on-call — дежурство
• pager — оповещение
• rollback — откат
• hotfix — горячее исправление
• mean time to recovery — среднее время восстановления
• blameless — без обвинений

keyVocabulary.example: We had a P1 incident last night.
The root cause was a misconfigured DNS.
We had to rollback the deployment.

grammarPoints: Контекст: Postmortem, root cause analysis, SLA breach

defaultCards.context: We had a P1 incident last night.
The root cause was a misconfigured DNS.
We had to rollback the deployment.

defaultCards.back: Postmortem, root cause analysis, SLA breach

defaultCards.context: We had a P1 incident last night.
The root cause was a misconfigured DNS.

title: Technical writing
subtitle: RFC, ADR, design documents
content:
Урок C1: Technical writing. RFC, ADR, design documents.

• RFC — запрос на комментарии
• ADR — запись архитектурного решения
• design doc — проектный документ
• trade-off — компромисс
• scope — объём, масштаб
• out of scope — за рамками
• acceptance criteria — критерии приёмки
• proof of concept — прототип, PoC
• technical specification — техническое задание
• requirements gathering — сбор требований

keyVocabulary.example: I wrote an RFC for the new auth system.
Document this decision as an ADR.
The design doc needs stakeholder review.
That feature is out of scope.
Let's build a proof of concept first.
The tech spec is ready for review.

defaultCards.context: I wrote an RFC for the new auth system.
Document this decision as an ADR.
The design doc needs stakeholder review.
That feature is out of scope.
Let's build a proof of concept first.
The tech spec is ready for review.
I wrote an RFC for the new auth system.
Document this decision as an ADR.
The design doc needs stakeholder review.

title: Advanced Agile
subtitle: Retrospective, velocity, burndown charts
content:
Урок C1: Advanced Agile. Retrospective, velocity, burndown charts.

• epic — эпик
• spike — спайк, исследование
• ceremony — церемония (Agile)

keyVocabulary.example: Let's discuss what went well in the retro.
Our velocity is 40 story points per sprint.
The burndown shows we're behind schedule.
How many story points is this task?
Does this meet the definition of done?
We need a backlog grooming session.
Let's create a spike to evaluate options.
Which Agile ceremonies do you follow?

Our velocity is 40 story points per sprint.
The burndown shows we're behind schedule.
How many story points is this task?
Does this meet the definition of done?
We need a backlog grooming session.
Let's create a spike to evaluate options.
Which Agile ceremonies do you follow?

title: DevOps & SRE
subtitle: Observability, error budget, toil
content:
Урок C1: DevOps & SRE. Observability, error budget, toil.

• observability — наблюдаемость
• error budget — бюджет ошибок
• toil — рутинная ручная работа
• SRE — инженер по надёжности
• SLO — цель уровня обслуживания
• SLI — индикатор уровня обслуживания

• runbook — рунбук, инструкция
• alert fatigue — усталость от оповещений
• golden signals — золотые сигналы

keyVocabulary.example: We've consumed 80% of our error budget.
Automate toil to free up engineering time.
Our SRE team manages production reliability.
Track SLIs to measure service health.
Follow the runbook for this alert.
Reduce alert fatigue by tuning thresholds.

defaultCards.context: We've consumed 80% of our error budget.
Automate toil to free up engineering time.
Our SRE team manages production reliability.
Track SLIs to measure service health.
Follow the runbook for this alert.
Reduce alert fatigue by tuning thresholds.
We've consumed 80% of our error budget.
Automate toil to free up engineering time.

keyVocabulary.example: Raw data goes to the data lake.

title: Performance optimization
subtitle: Latency, throughput, profiling

• indexing — индексирование
• N+1 query — N+1 запрос
• benchmark — бенчмарк
• flame graph — граф пламени
• optimization — оптимизация

keyVocabulary.example: Profile the application to find bottlenecks.
We detected a memory leak in production.
The flame graph shows CPU hotspots.
Premature optimization is the root of all evil.

defaultCards.context: Profile the application to find bottlenecks.
We detected a memory leak in production.
The flame graph shows CPU hotspots.
Premature optimization is the root of all evil.

• authentication — аутентификация

keyVocabulary.example: Add a new API endpoint for users.
The request payload is too large.
Use an idempotency key for payments.

• vulnerability — уязвимость

• two-factor authentication — двухфакторная аутентификация
• firewall — межсетевой экран
• audit log — журнал аудита
• access control — контроль доступа
• security patch — патч безопасности

keyVocabulary.example: All data must be encrypted at rest.

content:

• privacy policy — политика конфиденциальности
• consent — согласие
• data breach notification — уведомление об утечке
• right to be forgotten — право на забвение
• DPO — ответственный за защиту данных

keyVocabulary.example: GDPR applies to EU user data.
The annual security audit is next month.
Report a data breach within 72 hours.
